prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for formaldehyde (CH₂O, 4 atoms) using the fukui-reactivity skill with DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (PySCF RKS, density-fitted, tier 'standard'), in the gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 (cation: charge +1, mult 2; anion: charge −1, mult 2). Partial charges were computed via the Mulliken scheme. Geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file without re-optimization. All three SCFs (N, N+1, N−1) converged. Integrity: trustworthy = true. Warnings (all passed): sum(f+) = 1.0000; sum(f-) = 1.0000; common header present.

Key per-atom Fukui indices (f+, f-, f0, dual descriptor):
- O (atom 1): f+ = 0.46814, f- = 0.46742, f0 = 0.46778, Δf = +0.00071
- C (atom 2): f+ = 0.70839, f- = 0.11244, f0 = 0.41042, Δf = +0.59595  ← most electrophilic
- H (atom 3): f+ = −0.08827, f- = 0.21008, f0 = 0.06090, Δf = −0.29835
- H (atom 4): f+ = −0.08826, f- = 0.21006, f0 = 0.06090, Δf = −0.29832

Most electrophilic site: C (atom 2), f+ = 0.70839 (favoured for nucleophilic attack).
Most nucleophilic site: O (atom 1), f- = 0.46742 (favoured for electrophilic attack).
Headline value reported: f+ for carbon (the dominant electrophilic index) = 0.70839.
